etymology.description: 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread") + 㡭(㡭) (jì) = thread coming one after another -> continue; follow; succeed
etymology.interpretation: In 繼(繼), the component 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread") suggests continuity, resembling threads being woven continuously. The phonetic element 㡭(㡭) (jì) provides the sound, aligning with the idea of succeeding or following. The original form possibly depicted multiple threads, reinforcing the notion of continuation.
etymology.simplification: The character 繼 was simplified to 继 by replacing the 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread") with the simpler 纟, while retaining the phonetic value provided by 㡭(㡭).

examples.chinese: 他继承了父亲的事业(他繼承了父親的事業)。
examples.pinyin: Tā jìchéng le fùqīn de shìyè.

examples.chinese: 会议将在午餐后继续(會議將在午餐後繼續)。
examples.pinyin: Huìyì jiāng zài wǔcān hòu jìxù.
examples.english: The meeting will continue after lunch.

examples.chinese: 他们制定了一个继往开来的计划(他們制定了一個繼往開來的計劃)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen zhìdìng le yī gè jìwǎngkāilái de jìhuà.
examples.english: They devised a plan to continue and innovate.
